name: Schwyz (Switzerland)
description: A square red field with a small white couped cross in the upper hoist canton.
history: Schwyz used a plain red war banner (Blutbanner) from 1240; a crucifixion was added from the 14th century, reduced to the present couped cross in the canton in 1815, with its proportions fixed in 1963. The canton gave its name to Switzerland.

features.symbolism: the Christian cross, set in the upper-hoist canton
